title: MINUTE ChIP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dkbkj","text":"The need for quantitative ChIP-seq has been widely appreciated, and a number of quantitative methods have been proposed. Mint-ChIP, developed by the Bernstein lab provides a streamlined one-pot chromatin barcoding and a post ChIP linear amplification that requires only one adaptor per chromatin fragment. Here, we introduce unique molecule (unique molecular identifier [UMI]) counting and paired-end mapping of the chromatin fragments to this method, which we then termed MINUTEChIP for multiplexed indexed unique molecule T7 amplification end-to-end sequencing. For paired-end mapping, we modified the linear amplification strategy introduced in Mint-ChIP by priming the cDNA synthesis from a 30 adaptor (RA3) ligated onto the amplified RNA, thus maintaining the original genomic fragment length, retrieving atypical mononucleosomal fragment length for histone ChIP. We have also optimized the ligation conditions and stoichiometry of adaptors to chromatin to enable sequencing of the input. We show that MINUTE-ChIP is a sensitive method with a large linear dynamic range for accurately quantifying relative differences in genome-wide histone modification patterns across multiple pooled samples.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"qi3ba","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6l54o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6172D9D6341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":218,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"eausp\",\"text\":\"Schematic of MINUTE adaptor design for barcoding chromatin. One-sided ligation of adaptor comprising T7 RNA polymerase promoter (T7), random 6-bp sequence (UMI), and 8-bp barcode (BC) is sufficient for subsequent linear amplification. cDNA is primed from a ligated RNA adaptor (RA3). SBS3 and SBS12 designate standard Illumina read1 and read2 sequencing primers.\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"Screen Shot 2019-10-30 at 3.57.44 PM.png","placeholder":"","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/ab67304d0fec75ebfd89851646ff1c06fbc3769cef7bdba0163c5951dcb9b10d/btfa9xxw.png","width":646}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7jplk","text":"Optimal fragmentation of the genomic DNA is crucial for the success of the protocol. Hence, the concentration of MNase to be used needs to be titrated based on cell type and density in a pilot experiment. steps: Harvest 10^6 cells, spin, flash freeze the pellet. Make sure cell pellet is frozen with as little excess liquid as possible
On the day of experiment resuspend in 50 µL of ice cold PBS.

Add 50 µL of MNase mix (final 2U/ul in 2xLyB for mouse embryonic stem cells). MNase mix : First add 20 μ l protease inhibitor to 1 ml 2x lysis buffer (2xLyB) Stepwise dilute MNase (M0247S NEB 2000 U/ul ) to 20U/μl and then 2U/μl in 2x LyB Final working conc. = 2U/μl in 2xLyB.
Lyse cells on ice for 00:20:00 . Start preparing the enzyme mix for T7 adapter ligation.
Digest at 37 °C for 00:10:00 with agitation at 800 rpm.
Transfer tubes On ice
Prepare enzyme mix: %s %s
QUICKLY add 36.6 µL enzyme mix to 40 µL of the digested cells. Then add 4 µL of unique T7 adapter to each sample. Total volume ~80 μ l (40 μ l cells + 4 μ l T7 adapter + 36 μ l enzyme mix)
Next day, add 1x protease inhibitor (100x) to Lysis dilution buffer and add 80 µL of it to each sample in order to end reaction (Total volume per well: 160 µL )
Precouple antibody to the magnetic beads IP conditions such as amount of antibody to use, volume of pooled lysate to be added and duration of incubation can be optimized for individual experiments.
Was 50 µL Protein G/A beads with 2x 1000 µL PBS-T(0.1%)
Resuspend beads in 200 µL of the same buffer and incubate with appropriate amount of antibodies for 01:00:00 at Room temperature
Wash coupled beads quickly (<1 min) with 200 µL RIPA and resuspend in 50 µL of Lysis dilution buffer.
Top up with 200 - 400 μ l of the pooled lysate for each IP*, and rotate tube for 04:00:00 at 4 °C
Wash
Magnetize samples and save supernatant at -20ºC This supernatant can be used for checking IP efficiency or other troubleshooting

Resuspend, rotate for 5 mins, magnetize and aspirate : 2x with 500 μl ice cold RIPA buffer 1x with 500 μl ice cold RIPA/high salt buffer 2x with 500 μl ice cold LiCl wash buffer 1x with 200 μl ice cold TE (quick wash and change to new tube)
Transfer supernatant to a fresh tube. Remember to save appropriate amount of the pool as input ( Usually 5-10% of the volume used for the IPs)
Combine all samples into a single tube; spin down at 24000 rpm for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Invitro Transcription (IVT)
Prepare IVT mix in PCR tubes as follows: %s %s It is advisable to take as much ChIP DNA as possible into the IVT reaction to maximize the library diversity

Silane bead cleanup to isolate RNA (room temperature) Magnetize 15 µL of Silane beads per sample (will bind up to 3 μ g RNA). Wash with 60 µL buffer RLT, magnetize, aspirate and resuspend beads in 60 µL buffer RLT. Add 60 µL washed beads to 20 µL sample, wait 00:01:00 Add 48 µL 100% EtOH (final: 0.6 x ), mix well, wait 00:05:00 (mix by pipetting at 2.5 min) Magnetize, wash twice with 200 µL 70% EtOH Air-dry at RT for atleast 00:05:00 RNA eluted in 10 µL EB Measure 1 µL on Qubit
Digest at 63 °C for 01:00:00 (native chromatin) or overnight (to reverse crosslink fixed chromatin).
Resuspend chromatin/antibody/beads in 97.5 µL of ChIP elution buffer and 2.5 µL of Proteinase K. Remember to process the INPUT sample collected in Step 9.1, similar to the IPs from this step on.
Elution from protein G beads and protein digestion
RNA 3’ adapter ligation
Set up the ligation rxn as follows per sample: %s %s
Incubate at 25 °C for 02:00:00 .
Reverse Transcription
Move tube to ice and top up with the following components of the reverse transcription reaction: %s %s Heat to 65 °C for 00:05:00 and place on ice for 00:01:00
Then add : %s %s Incubate in a thermocycler with following program : %s %s
Add 1 µL Rnase H, DNase free and incubate 00:15:00 at 37 °C . (this helps for accurate cDNA measurements)
Library PCR
And set it up with the following cycling conditions: %s %s

SPRI bead cleanup to isolate DNA (room temperature) We employed 1x to select for all >100bp fragments; no specific size selection. But one can also size select at this step. Add 50 µL of nuclease free water to the PCR mixture to make final volume 100 µL . Add 100 µL SPRI beads (1x select >100 bp fragments) Mix by pipetting, wait 00:10:00 Magnetize for 00:05:00 Remove supernatant Wash 2x with 200 µL 70% ethanol (magnetize 1 minute) Air-dry at RT for >= 00:05:00 Elute DNA in 20 µL of elution buffer. Measure 1 µL on Qubit.
Adjust cDNA conc. for all samples to be 150 ng (or a minimum of 100 ng) in a total volume of 22.5 µL with nuclease free water and set up following PCR reaction mix : %s %s

SPRI bead cleanup to isolate DNA (room temperature) Add 59 µL of nuclease free water to the cDNA mixture to make final volume 1 00 µL Add 100 µL SPRI beads (1x select >100 bp fragments) Mix by pipetting, wait 00:10:00 Magnetize for 00:05:00 Remove supernatant Wash 2x with 200 µL 70% ethanol (magnetize 1 minute) Air-dry at RT for >= 00:05:00 Elute DNA in 24 µL of TE and measure conc. on Nanodrop.
Incubate samples at 37 °C overnight 16:00:00, roughly

SPRI bead cleanup to isolate DNA (room temperature) Using magnet, transfer 100 µL eluate to new wells. Add 100 µL SPRI beads (1x, select >100 bp fragments) Mix by pipetting, wait 00:10:00 Magnetize for 00:05:00 Remove supernatant Wash 2x with 200 µL 70% ethanol (magnetize 1 minute) Air-dry at RT for >= 00:05:00 Elute DNA in 12 µL EB and measure 1 µL with Qubit There is no 'right' amount of ChIP DNA. Selective antibodies against low abundant targets will yield little DNA

Mix and incubate with agitation at 800 rpm at Room temperature for 02:00:00 and then at 16 °C overnight. The manufacturer's protocol for the end repair and blunt end ligation suggests 60 minutes incubation at room temperature.We have obtained satisfactory results with just 2 hours incubation at room temperature. The overnight incubation is therefore optional and was introduced more as a break point for the protocol.
creator: Banushree Kumar
